content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
December 13, 2021 
Nick Warshaw 
Olson Remcho 
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 400 
Sacramento, CA 95814-4503 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-21-165 
Dear Mr. Warshaw: 
This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the “Act”).1 
QUESTION 
May the candidates and committees involved in the Assembly District 17 February 15, 2022 
special primary election combine the first pre-election statement with the second pre-election 
statement due on February 3, 2022? 
CONCLUSIONS 
In connection with a February 15, 2022 special primary election, candidates and committees 
may use a filing schedule that combines the first pre-election statement with the second pre-election 
statement. The combined statement will be due on February 3, 2022, covering the period January 1, 
2022, through January 29, 2022. 
ANALYSIS 
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are required to file 
two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in 
connection with that election. These pre-election campaign statements are generally filed 40 days 
before the election and again 12 days before the election. (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual 
statements are filed January 31 and July 31, respectively. (Section 84200.) Pursuant to Section 
84205, the Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to 
file combined campaign statements and reports required by the Act. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18109 through 18998 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


 
  
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
   
File No. A-21-165 
Page No. 2 
A special election has been called for Assembly District 17, with the primary election being 
held on February 15, 2022. Committees required to file semi-annual statements will file a report 
covering through December 31, 2021. Thus, to comply with the Act’s pre-election report 
requirements in conjunction with the special election, candidates and committees would be required 
to file a first pre-election report covering one day, January 1, 2022; and then a second pre-election 
report covering January 2 – January 29, 2022. The first pre-election report would be due January 6, 
2022; while the second pre-election report would be due February 3, 2022. 
Given the timing of the election and the fact that this would render a first pre-election report 
covering a one-day period, the Commission will permit candidates and committees required to file 
to combine the first and second pre-election reports. The period covered will be January 1 – January 
29, 2022, and the report will be due February 3, 2022. Combining these statements will streamline 
filing for those participating in the election, while also easing the burden of processing and review 
on behalf of filing agencies. With the reports being combined, there will be a delay in disclosure of 
activity occurring on January 1. However, as this is a holiday, and the activity will still be disclosed 
well in advance of the election, the benefits to combining outweigh any potential delay.2 
Filing the single pre-election statements in connection with the February 15 election will 
satisfy both pre-election report requirements under 84200.8. A campaign statement filing schedule 
which outlines the due dates for filing statements in connection with the election is enclosed for 
your convenience. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Erika M. Boyd 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
EMB:dkv 
CC: 
Political Reform Division, Secretary of Stat
San Francisco Ethics Commission 
e 
2 Additionally, the 90-day late contribution reporting period for the special primary began on November 17, 
2021. During this time, contributions and independent expenditures aggregating $1,000 or more must be reported within 
24-hours.

2022, through January 29, 2022.
